title: clematis dioica subsp. campestris
intro: Clematis dioica subsp. campestris, also known as woodland clematis or American virginsbower, is a perennial climbing plant native to eastern North America, characterized by its delicate white flowers and blue-purple berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties, which help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps. The health benefits of clematis dioica subsp. campestris include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting relaxation, making it a popular herbal remedy for women's health issues. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
